description: Repository-level MCP catalog for AI agents. Each entry documents what the MCP does, how to configure it, and where it is used.

mcps.name: Linear MCP
mcps.purpose: Connect AI agents to Linear for issue/project workflows.

clientExamples.claude: claude mcp add --transport http linear https://mcp.linear.app/mcp

aiHints: Prefer scoped queries to reduce token usage
Do not fetch broad payloads when a narrow query is enough

mcps.name: Notion MCP
mcps.purpose: Read Notion pages/databases as operational memory for AI prompts and workflows.

notes: Keep Notion workspace and integration scopes minimal
Use read-only access unless write is required

aiHints: If Notion MCP is unavailable, fail fast and ask for setup
Summarize retrieved content before injecting into AGENTS.md

mcps.name: XMind MCP
mcps.purpose: Create, read, and query XMind files for planning and reporting.
aiHints: Write JSON plan first, then call create_xmind
Restrict file operations to allowed directories

mcps.name: ChatPRD MCP
mcps.purpose: Connect IDE/AI clients to ChatPRD for reading documents and creating PRDs.
capabilities: Use ChatPRD tools without leaving development workflow

notes: Integration is marked Pro Only in ChatPRD
Use client-specific MCP settings/deeplink from ChatPRD integrations page

aiHints: Use ChatPRD MCP when requirement context is in ChatPRD
Prefer concise retrieval before generating implementation plans
